summary.title: Student Exam Management System Threat Model
summary.description: Threat model for a hypothetical web-based exam platform supporting student exam taking, lecturer exam authoring and grading, and administrator user and audit management. Federated authentication via a school identity provider

diagrams.title: Level 2 — Submission and Grading
diagrams.placeholder: Level 2 decomposition of P3 Submission Handler and P4 Grading.

data.name: P3 Submission Handler — decomposition (service tier)
data.description: Grade Publisher reads the confirmed final grade from DS3b. Read-only access; triggers a publication audit event.

data.name: Student
data.description: External user. Covered at Level 1. Included here for submission and grade-publish flow context only.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Covered at Level 1; included here for flow context only.

data.name: Lecturer
data.description: External staff user. Covered at Level 1. Included here for manual-grade entry flow context only.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Covered at Level 1; included here for flow context only.

data.name: P3.1 Submission Receiver
data.description: HTTP endpoint that accepts POST /submit from the student over HTTPS. Receives the raw JSON payload. No validation at this stage — all input must be treated as untrusted. Hands the raw payload directly to P3.2 for validation.

threats.title: Session cookie theft enabling exam impersonation
threats.description: A student's session cookie is stolen via XSS in the exam interface, a compromised browser extension, or shoulder-surfing on a shared machine. The attacker replays the cookie to POST /submit on behalf of the victim, or reads in-progress answers.
threats.mitigation: HttpOnly and Secure flags on session cookies; short TTL (≤ exam window duration); Content Security Policy preventing XSS; token binding or SameSite=Strict; rotate session token on any privilege change.

data.name: P3.2 Submission Validator
data.description: Validates the raw payload against the exam schema, checks the submission window is open, verifies the student owns the exam seat, and sanitises free-text input. The trust-elevation point: input north of this process is untrusted; south is structurally validated. Passes the validated payload to P3.3.

threats.title: Submission replay — student resubmits after deadline
threats.description: A student records a valid POST /submit request made before the deadline and replays it after the window closes to replace a poor answer set with a better one. P3.1 does not check the submission timestamp against the server clock; it trusts the client-supplied timestamp.
threats.mitigation: P3.2 validates the submission window server-side against a server clock, not the client timestamp; the submission payload includes a server-issued nonce that is single-use and expires at the window close; P3.3 stores a server-generated timestamp; any submission after window-close is rejected with a 403 and an audit event.

data.name: P3.3 Submission Persister
data.description: Persists the validated submission to DS3a with a server-generated timestamp and a SHA-256 content hash (tamper evidence). Returns a submission acknowledgement to the student. Appends an audit event to DS3c.

data.name: Auto-Grader
data.description: Reads pending submissions from DS3a and the answer key from DS2. Applies the MCQ/short-answer matching logic to compute a provisional mark. Writes the provisional mark to DS3b. Appends a grading audit event to DS3c.

data.name: Manual-Grade Entry
data.description: Accepts lecturer-authenticated HTTP requests to enter or update a manual mark for an open-ended answer. Writes the mark to DS3b. Appends an audit event to DS3c recording the lecturer identity, mark, and timestamp.

threats.title: Manual-grade entry without re-authentication (P4.2 privilege escalation)
threats.description: A lecturer's session token is long-lived. An attacker who has obtained the token (via XSS or interception) can submit manual marks through P4.2 without triggering a re-authentication challenge, elevating the effective privilege of their access to grade-writing.
threats.mitigation: Require step-up authentication (re-entry of credentials or MFA prompt) before any grade write via P4.2; session tokens for grade-entry actions have a 15-minute inactivity timeout; all grade writes are logged in DS3c with the session token fingerprint and originating IP.

data.name: P4.3 Grade Publisher
data.description: Reads the finalised grade from DS3b once all marks are confirmed. Publishes the grade to the student over HTTPS. Appends a publication audit event to DS3c. Read-only against DS3b; write-only against DS3c.

data.name: DS3a — Pending Submissions
data.description: Internal queue store holding raw validated submissions awaiting grading. Contains student answer payloads and content hashes. Encrypted at rest; accessible to P3.3 (write) and P4.1 (read). Cleared after grading is finalised.

data.name: DS3b — Final Grades
data.description: Authoritative grade record. Stores provisional marks from P4.1, manual marks from P4.2, and the confirmed final grade. Encrypted at rest. Write access is restricted to P4.1 and P4.2; P4.3 has read-only access.

threats.title: Grade tampering via direct DS3b write bypassing the grading workflow
threats.description: An attacker who has compromised the grading service account (P4.1) can issue arbitrary UPDATE or INSERT statements against DS3b, writing any grade without going through the normal workflow. There is no secondary approval mechanism for grade changes once the service account has database write access.
threats.mitigation: The grading service account is restricted to a stored procedure interface — no direct table UPDATE rights; grade changes trigger a mandatory audit event in DS3c; the stored procedure validates that the mark is within the allowed range and that the exam window has closed; dual-control flag for any post-publish grade change.

data.name: DS3c — Grade Audit Chain
data.description: Append-only, hash-linked audit log for the submission and grading zone. Each entry includes actor identity, action, timestamp, resource reference, and the hash of the previous entry. The application service accounts have INSERT only — no UPDATE or DELETE. Signed to detect tampering.

data.name: DS2 — Exam Bank (read-only here)
data.description: Re-drawn from Level 1 for context. At Level 2, P4.1 reads the answer key from this store. The grading service account has SELECT on answer keys only; no write access at this level.

threats.title: Pre-exam answer key exposure via unauthorised DS2 read
threats.description: A student or compromised low-privilege service account reads the answer key from DS2 before the exam window opens. The grading service account used by P4.1 has broader SELECT rights than it needs, making this a viable pivot target.
threats.mitigation: Column-level access control: the grading service account has SELECT on answer-key rows only after the exam window closes; answer keys are stored encrypted with a key that is only accessible to P4.1 at grading time; all answer-key reads produce audit events in DS3c; log and alert on any out-of-window read attempt.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Manual-Grade Entry appends a mark audit event: lecturer identity, exam ID, mark, and timestamp.

data.name: read final grade
data.description: Grade Publisher reads the confirmed final grade from DS3b. Read-only access; triggers a publication audit event.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Grade Publisher appends a publication audit event: student ID, exam ID, final grade, and publication timestamp.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Auto-Grader appends a grading audit event: exam ID, provisional mark, answer-key version, and timestamp.

data.name: read answer key
data.description: Auto-Grader reads the published answer key from DS2. Read-only; restricted to answer key rows for the specific exam. Access window enforced at the application layer.

data.name: read pending submission
data.description: Auto-Grader reads pending validated submissions from DS3a for processing. SELECT only; no write access.

data.name: write provisional mark
data.description: Auto-Grader writes a provisional mark to DS3b via a stored procedure. No direct table UPDATE rights.

data.name: write submission
data.description: Persists the validated submission record to DS3a with a server timestamp and SHA-256 content hash.

data.name: append audit event
data.description: Submission Persister appends an audit event to DS3c: student ID, exam ID, timestamp, content hash, and correlation ID.

data.name: submission acknowledgement
data.description: HTTP 200 confirmation returned to the student: submission received, timestamp, and correlation ID. No payload details included.

data.name: submit answers (HTTPS)
data.description: Student submits completed exam answers as a JSON payload over HTTPS. Crosses the Internet boundary. Raw and untrusted at this point.

data.name: enter manual mark
data.description: Lecturer submits an open-ended mark via authenticated HTTPS request. Requires step-up authentication.

data.name: publish grade
data.description: Finalised grade delivered to the student over HTTPS. No raw mark breakdown included by default; only the confirmed result.

data.name: raw payload
data.description: Unvalidated student submission payload passed from the HTTP receiver to the validator. This flow crosses the L2 trust boundary — everything north is untrusted, everything south is validated.

threats.title: Verbose validator error leaks payload fragments
threats.description: When a malformed JSON submission reaches P3.2, the validator throws a parser exception whose default error message includes a fragment of the offending payload. The error is returned to the client in the HTTP response, leaking part of the submission text. In a multi-tenant exam where students cannot see each other's answers, this is a confidentiality break.
threats.mitigation: Catch parser exceptions explicitly in P3.2; return a generic 400 Bad Request with a correlation ID only; log the full error and raw payload server-side only; suppress all stack traces and exception messages from HTTP responses across the application.

data.name: validated payload
data.description: Structurally validated and sanitised submission payload, safe to persist. Crosses into trusted processing.

data.name: write manual mark
data.description: Manual mark written to DS3b via a stored procedure interface. Triggers a mandatory audit event in DS3c.

diagrams.description: Level 2 decomposition of P3 (Submission Handler) and P4 (Grading). Exposes six sub-processes, four internal data stores, and the critical trust boundary between P3.1 (untrusted input receiver) and P3.2 (validated input). Student and Lecturer are marked out of scope — they are fully covered at Level 1.
